title: Frankincense and cedarwood room spray
subtitle: A resinous, woody room spray for quiet spaces
excerpt: A room spray made with isopropyl alcohol, frankincense and cedarwood essential oils, and a small amount of distilled water. Spritz into the room or onto fabric cushions and curtains for a warm, woody fragrance.
sourceNotes: Isopropyl alcohol as a room-spray carrier: evaporates quickly leaving only the essential oil fragrance. Ethanol is preferable for fabric-contact sprays but IPA at 70% is the widely available household equivalent. Essential oil safe room-spray concentrations: 5 to 10% of formula weight for a moderate room throw. Frankincense and cedarwood both have high flash points (above 60°C), suitable for spray use.

recipe.yieldDescription: 1 spray bottle (~100 ml, approximately 60 to 80 sprays)
recipe.batchNotes: Scale proportionally. Essential oil content stays at 8% of formula weight.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Ready to use immediately. Shake before each use.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Store in the spray bottle away from heat and direct sunlight. Keeps 12 months. The alcohol base is a natural preservative. Shake before each use as the essential oils may settle.

glossaryTerms.definition: The temperature at which an essential oil can ignite near a flame. Frankincense and cedarwood both have flash points above 60°C; the isopropyl alcohol carrier is the more flammable component of this spray.

content.text: An alcohol-based room spray works by dispersing a fine mist of essential oil in fast-evaporating alcohol. The alcohol carries the oils into the air, then evaporates. Frankincense brings a warm, resinous note; cedarwood is dry and woody. Both have

values above 60°C, well above room temperature. The flammable component in this spray is the isopropyl alcohol. Keep away from naked flames. The small water addition stops the spray feeling completely dry on fabric. Use in living rooms, hallways, or any room where a calm, woody fragrance is wanted.

items.prepNote: 75% of formula; 70% or 99% IPA both work; the essential oils dissolve in either
17% of formula; added last, after the essential oils are dissolved
3% of formula; dry woody base note

content.text: Add the essential oils to the spray bottle first. Add the isopropyl alcohol and shake to dissolve the oils fully in the alcohol. Add the distilled water last and shake again. Label with the contents and date. The spray is ready to use immediately.

Shake before each use. Spray 3 to 4 times into the centre of the room from about 1 metre up to allow the mist to disperse before settling. Can also be spritzed onto fabric cushion covers, curtains, or bed linen from a 30 to 40 cm distance; the alcohol evaporates in seconds and the resinous fragrance lingers. Do not spray onto polished wood surfaces or painted walls; the alcohol can affect the finish. Keep away from naked flames.
Store away from heat and sunlight. The alcohol is a natural preservative; the spray keeps 12 months. Shake before each use.
